seoTitle: How to Generate Reports in Zendesk Using Explore
metaDescription: Build support performance reports in Zendesk Explore: pick a dataset, stack metrics against attributes, filter by date, and add charts to a dashboard.
intro: Guessing at your team's response times or ticket volume isn't a substitute for real data. This guide shows how to use Zendesk Explore to select the right dataset, pair metrics with attributes like agent name, apply filters, and assemble the results into a shareable dashboard.

steps.heading: Launch Zendesk Explore
steps.body: Open the Products menu and select Analytics to access the Explore reporting workspace.

steps.heading: Pick your dataset
steps.body: Start a new report and choose the core dataset, such as Support Tickets, that matches what you want to measure.

steps.heading: Add your metrics
steps.body: Go to the Metrics panel and add what you want to count, like ticket volume, to the report.

steps.heading: Add attributes to slice the data
steps.body: Drop an attribute such as assignee name into the columns or rows to break the metric down by team member or category.

steps.heading: Choose a chart type and filter
steps.body: Switch between table, bar, or line layouts, then apply date range filters before saving your query to the library.

steps.heading: Build a dashboard
steps.body: Use the dashboard builder to embed your saved chart widgets into a single view for stakeholders.

faq.question: What's the difference between a metric and an attribute in Explore?
faq.answer: A metric is what you're counting or measuring, like ticket count, while an attribute is how you group or slice that number, like by agent.

faq.question: Can I schedule reports to be emailed automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, Zendesk Explore dashboards can be set up for scheduled email delivery so leadership gets recurring updates.

faq.question: Do I need a specific plan to access Explore?
faq.answer: Reporting features and depth of access can vary by Zendesk subscription tier, so check your plan for full details.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple charts into one dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, the dashboard builder is designed to let you place several saved reports onto a single shared view.
